title: Sour Light German Berries Flatbread
description: A quick, Jain-friendly German flatbread bursting with tart saskatoon berries, baked to a crisp finish in a tandoorstyle oven for a crowdpleasing dessert.
instructions: Preheat your oven to 220 °C (425 °F) and place a baking stone or steel inside to heat; if you have a tandoor, preheat it to 230 °C (450 °F).
In a small bowl, dissolve the yeast in the warm water with a pinch of sugar; let it sit 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, salt, remaining sugar, and vinegar until combined.
Add the yeast mixture and olive oil to the dry ingredients and knead until a soft, elastic dough forms; cover with a damp cloth and let rise for 20 minutes.
Poke the dough to release air, then gently press it into a round flatbread shape, about 30 cm in diameter.
Spread a thin layer of vegan yogurt over the surface, then scatter the saskatoon berries evenly across the top.
Transfer the flatbread to the preheated stone or steel and bake for 12–14 minutes, or until the edges are golden and the surface curls slightly; if using a tandoor, cook for 10–12 minutes.
Remove from heat, brush lightly with olive oil, and sprinkle lemon zest and mint leaves for aroma and freshness.
Let cool for 5 minutes before slicing into wedges; serve warm as a sweet or savory dessert.
Store leftovers in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
